title: Freehand: tell me about your topic
testCase: Are there headings, paragraphs and at least one list?
blocks.2.text: Write a page about your topic that actually tells something. Not one line — a whole page someone would read to the end.
blocks.3.text: It must have: a main heading, at least two sections with sub-headings, paragraphs, one list of something (reasons, favourites, steps — whatever fits) and some emphasis where it makes sense.
blocks.4.text: I am not giving you a skeleton. I am not giving you an order. You decide what goes in and in what sequence.
blocks.6.text: It will look ugly. Serif font, everything in one column, no colours. You did nothing wrong — that is what a bare page without CSS looks like. The looks come in the next course.
blocks.7.text: Many people quit right here, because they think that if it is ugly, they must have made a mistake. You have not. You simply have not reached the beauty part yet.
